title: Rewired co-host portrait · Jaye Anne Beringer re-mediumed in Feral Architecture palette
variants:
You are being given ONE reference image: a photographic headshot of a woman (Jaye Anne Beringer) with curly shoulder-length hair in a warm brown base with bronze and caramel highlights, large round tortoiseshell-frame eyeglasses which she is pulling slightly downward with her right hand (blue-polished fingernails visible, bracelet on wrist), peeking playfully OVER the top of the glasses directly at the viewer, wearing a multi-colored plaid blazer (red, blue, pink, yellow, green windowpane plaid), a cream-colored blouse underneath, and a pearl necklace at her collar. Her expression is playful, curious, one-eyebrow-raised, quietly teasing — amused intelligence, not spectacle. The reference background is a plain neutral gray studio backdrop.

TASK: Render this exact person as a dark graphic novel / occult editorial plate author portrait in the Feral Architecture brand visual style. This is the co-host portrait for the Rewired podcast website. Maintain her LIKENESS with precision — face structure, curly hair shape and density, glasses shape and position being held down, the gesture of pulling the glasses, the playful peek-over-the-glasses expression, pearl necklace, and blazer silhouette must all be clearly recognizably HER. Do not idealize, do not beautify, do not alter her features, do not 'age her backward,' do not slim her face. This is a re-medium of her existing photograph, not a reimagining of what she could look like.

KEEP EXACTLY FROM THE REFERENCE: face structure and proportions, curly shoulder-length hair (warm brown with bronze/caramel highlights, natural curl pattern preserved), large round tortoiseshell-frame eyeglasses positioned partway down the nose bridge, right hand visible at the lower rim of the glasses with blue-painted nails and a bracelet on the wrist, the playful peek-over-the-glasses gaze directly at the viewer, the one-eyebrow-raised quietly-teasing expression, pearl necklace at the collar, the PATTERN and cut of the multi-colored plaid blazer (windowpane plaid, blazer silhouette, lapels), cream-colored blouse underneath, head tilt and shoulder position.

CHANGE — RE-MEDIUM TO FERAL ARCHITECTURE: Render the entire image as an illustrated dark graphic novel / occult editorial plate, not as a photograph. Replace the gray studio backdrop with a Void Purple #1a0f2e void that dissolves into atmospheric darkness at the edges. Replace the even studio lighting with strong Feral Architecture chiaroscuro: a primary cool Ion Glow cyan #5be7ff directional light source raking from the viewer's upper-left across her left cheek, the left side of her hair, the left lapel of the blazer, and catching a cool highlight in her left eye; a secondary warm Neon Magenta #ff3cac rim light from the viewer's right catching the right edge of her face, the right side of her curls, the right rim of the glasses, the curve of the pearl necklace, the bracelet on her wrist, and a warm catchlight in her right eye. The background dissolves into Void Purple darkness with Electric Violet #7a2cff atmospheric depth in the deepest corners. A faint chalked partial sigil of broken geometric circles and wound-seam arcs is barely visible on the Void Purple background behind her left shoulder — echoing the publication's visual grammar (do NOT render a literal logo, do NOT write any text anywhere in the image).

TRANSLATE THE BLAZER INTO THE ARCÆON FAMILY: The reference blazer is multi-colored plaid in red / blue / pink / yellow / green. Re-render this plaid as an ARCÆON-family plaid — the SAME windowpane pattern and blazer silhouette, but with the plaid colors remapped to the brand: Neon Magenta #ff3cac, Electric Violet #7a2cff, Ion Glow cyan #5be7ff, and a warm accent of Fusion Gold #ffb347 as the secondary windowpane lines, over a Void Purple base. The blazer should still clearly read as her signature colorful plaid — the pattern and silhouette are preserved — but the specific hues are now the ARCÆON palette. This is the ONLY element that gets color-translated; everything else (hair, skin, glasses, pearls, blouse) stays true to her actual coloring.

COLOR PALETTE: Void Purple #1a0f2e base (background, the deepest shadows under the jaw, inside the hair mass, behind the blazer lapels). Ion Glow cyan #5be7ff primary light (left side of face, left temple, catchlight in the left eye, edge light on the left side of the glasses frame, selective curls on the left). Neon Magenta #ff3cac secondary light (right rim on face, right curls, highlights on the pearl necklace and bracelet, catchlight in the right eye, warm edge on the right glasses rim). Electric Violet #7a2cff depth accents (atmospheric shadow in the background, deepest folds of the blazer, shadow behind the neck). Fusion Gold #ffb347 used ONLY as the secondary windowpane lines in the blazer plaid and as a warm undertone in the skin's lit planes. Nothing outside this family. Nothing. Ever.

MEDIUM: Dark graphic novel illustration meets occult editorial plate author portrait. Inked linework with painterly color fills. Confident, stylized portrait rendering with anatomical assurance. Painted chiaroscuro as shapes of light and shadow, not photographic gradients. Limited Feral Architecture palette. Selective detail — face and eyes richly rendered with painterly brushwork, curly hair rendered with confident linework and selective color (curl pattern preserved as painted shape language, not traced strand-by-strand), glasses rendered as a specific tortoiseshell-frame object with painted light on the rims, pearls rendered as discrete catchlit spheres, blazer rendered as confident painted plaid shapes, right hand at the glasses rendered with anatomical care (it's part of her signature gesture). The image should read as a single illustrated plate from a dark graphic novel or an indie tarot deck author portrait, NOT as a photograph, not as a photo filter, not as a 3D render.

STYLE REFERENCES (as anchors, not to mimic): the heavy-black ritual gravitas of Mike Mignola's Hellboy interiors, the occult-literate painterly portrait work of Dave McKean's Sandman covers, the confident editorial illustration of Tomer Hanuka, and the modern indie tarot deck author-portrait aesthetic.

EXPLICITLY NOT: not photorealistic, not a photo filter pass, not a 3D render, not manga or anime, not cartoon, not loose sketch, not a stock 'wise woman' or 'witchy librarian' fantasy portrait, not glowing supernatural eyes, not a tarot card with a frame or title bar, not a character illustration of 'a character who looks like her' — must be clearly HER, the specific person in the reference, just rendered in a different medium. Not beautified, not idealized, not aged backward. The playful peek-over-the-glasses gesture is LOAD-BEARING — it is a signature element of her expression and must be preserved exactly as in the reference. Should feel hand-crafted, illustrated, painted, with visible line work and painterly shape language.

COMPOSITION: square 1:1 aspect ratio, centered head-and-shoulders portrait, same framing as the reference photograph. The face at the upper-center, the peeking-over-glasses gesture fully visible, shoulders and blazer lapels at the lower edge, clear background on both sides for the brand palette to live. No text on the image. No frame. No border. No title overlay. No watermark.
